User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FinIntel AI platform, storing profile information and linking to all user-specific data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and as a unique login identifier.

username.description: A unique display name or handle for the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the user's profile information.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture or avatar.

web3WalletAddress.description: The user's Web3 wallet address, if connected (e.g., MetaMask).

LearningModule.title: LearningModule
LearningModule.description: Represents a structured section or category of financial learning content (e.g., 'Stock Market Basics').

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningModule entity.

title.description: The title of the learning module.

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

order.description: The sequential order of the module within the overall learning path.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents an individual lesson within a learning module, containing educational content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

learningModuleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N Lesson)

content.description: The main educational content of the lesson, possibly in Markdown or HTML format.

order.description: The sequential order of the lesson within its learning module.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents a quiz associated with a lesson to test user comprehension.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this quiz is associated with. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Quiz)

description.description: A brief description or instructions for the quiz.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents an individual question within a quiz, including options and the correct answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz this question belongs to. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The text of the quiz question.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for the question.

correctOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index of the correct answer within the 'options' array.

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a specific user's progress for a particular lesson, including completion status and quiz scores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N UserLessonProgress)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserLessonProgress)

status.description: The current status of the user's progress for the lesson ('not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed').

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the lesson.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp when the user last accessed or interacted with this lesson.

quizScore.description: The score obtained by the user on the quiz associated with this lesson (e.g., percentage or number of correct answers).

UserModuleProgress.title: UserModuleProgress
UserModuleProgress.description: Aggregates a user's progress across an entire learning module, providing an overall completion percentage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserModuleProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N UserModuleProgress)

learningModuleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N UserModuleProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The overall completion percentage for the module (0-100).

completed.description: Indicates if the entire learning module has been completed by the user.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the entire module.

Stock.title: Stock
Stock.description: Represents basic metadata for a financial stock or security.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Stock entity, typically its ticker symbol (e.g., 'AAPL').

symbol.description: The ticker symbol of the stock (e.g., 'AAPL').

companyName.description: The full company name associated with the stock.

exchange.description: The stock exchange where the stock is traded (e.g., 'NASDAQ', 'NYSE').

currency.description: The currency in which the stock is traded (e.g., 'USD').

sector.description: The industry sector the company belongs to (e.g., 'Technology', 'Finance').

industry.description: The specific industry within its sector (e.g., 'Software - Infrastructure', 'Investment Banking & Brokerage').

StockPrediction.title: StockPrediction
StockPrediction.description: Records a user's stock prediction, the AI's prediction, and the actual market outcome for a specific stock.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockPrediction event.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the prediction. (Relationship: User 1:N StockPrediction)

stockId.description: Reference to the Stock being predicted (using its symbol as ID). (Relationship: Stock 1:N StockPrediction)

predictionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the user and AI predictions were made.

predictionHorizon.description: The time frame over which the prediction applies (e.g., '1-day', '1-week', '1-month').

userPredictedDirection.description: The direction predicted by the user ('UP', 'DOWN', 'FLAT').

userConfidence.description: The user's confidence level in their prediction (0-100%).

aiPredictedDirection.description: The direction predicted by the AI model ('UP', 'DOWN', 'FLAT').

aiPredictionProbability.description: The probability score of the AI's prediction (0 to 1).

actualDirection.description: The actual market movement direction observed after the prediction horizon ('UP', 'DOWN', 'FLAT').

actualOutcomeTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the actual market outcome was determined.

userPredictionMatched.description: Indicates if the user's prediction matched the actual market outcome.

aiPredictionMatched.description: Indicates if the AI's prediction matched the actual market outcome.

aiExplanation.description: AI-generated explanation for why the prediction (user's or AI's) matched or failed.

startPrice.description: The stock price at the beginning of the prediction horizon.

endPrice.description: The stock price at the end of the prediction horizon.

NewsArticle.title: NewsArticle
NewsArticle.description: Stores information about a financial news article, including sentiment analysis results.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsArticle entity.

title.description: The headline or title of the news article.

source.description: The source or publisher of the news article (e.g., 'Reuters', 'Bloomberg').

url.description: The URL to the full news article.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the news article was published.

sentiment.description: The sentiment of the news article ('Positive', 'Negative', 'Neutral').

sentimentScore.description: A numerical score representing the sentiment intensity (e.g., -1.0 for very negative to 1.0 for very positive).

marketImpactScore.description: A score indicating the potential market impact of the news (e.g., 1-10).

summary.description: An AI-generated summary of the news article content.

topics.description: An array of keywords or topics associated with the news article.

Portfolio.title: Portfolio
Portfolio.description: Represents a user's investment portfolio, a collection of financial holdings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Portfolio entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this portfolio. (Relationship: User 1:N Portfolio)

name.description: A user-defined name for the portfolio (e.g., 'My Main Portfolio', 'Retirement Fund').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the portfolio details or holdings.

Holding.title: Holding
Holding.description: Represents a single asset (e.g., a stock) held within a user's portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Holding entity.

portfolioId.description: Reference to the Portfolio this holding belongs to. (Relationship: Portfolio 1:N Holding)

stockId.description: Reference to the Stock being held (using its symbol as ID). (Relationship: Stock 1:N Holding)

quantity.description: The number of units (shares) of the stock held.

averageCostBasis.description: The average price at which the shares were acquired, used for profit/loss calculation.

purchaseDate.description: The date when the holding was first acquired.

PortfolioAnalysis.title: PortfolioAnalysis
PortfolioAnalysis.description: Stores a snapshot of AI-generated analysis and insights for a user's portfolio at a specific point in time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioAnalysis entity.

portfolioId.description: Reference to the Portfolio being analyzed. (Relationship: Portfolio 1:N PortfolioAnalysis)

analysisDate.description: Timestamp when the portfolio analysis was performed.

totalValue.description: The total market value of all holdings in the portfolio at the time of analysis.

assetAllocationJson.description: JSON string representing asset allocation, e.g., '[{"assetType": "Stocks", "percentage": 70.0}, {"assetType": "Bonds", "percentage": 20.0}]'.

riskExposureScore.description: A calculated score indicating the portfolio's overall risk exposure (e.g., 1-10).

sectorDiversificationJson.description: JSON string representing sector diversification, e.g., '[{"sector": "Technology", "percentage": 30.0}, {"sector": "Finance", "percentage": 25.0}]'.

performanceMetricsJson.description: JSON string representing key performance indicators for the portfolio, e.g., '{"ytdReturn": 0.15, "lastMonthReturn": 0.02}'.

suggestedImprovements.description: AI-generated suggestions for improving the portfolio's performance or risk profile.

aiInsights.description: A comprehensive AI-generated summary of portfolio insights and observations.

ChatConversation.title: ChatConversation
ChatConversation.description: Represents a single conversation thread with the AI Financial Strategy Advisor chatbot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatConversation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this conversation. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatConversation)

title.description: An optional, AI-generated summary title for the conversation thread (e.g., 'Retirement Planning', 'Market Volatility').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last message exchanged in the conversation.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a ChatConversation with the AI advisor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the ChatConversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatConversation 1:N ChatMessage)

sender.description: Indicates who sent the message ('user' or 'ai').

content.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Accessible only by the user specified in the path, i.e., `request.auth.uid == userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Tracks a specific user's progress for a given lesson. Accessible only by the user specified in the path. Includes denormalized 'learningModuleId' for authorization independence and efficient lookup.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson this progress tracks.

definition.description: Aggregates a user's progress across an entire learning module. Accessible only by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the learning module this progress tracks.

definition.description: Records a user's stock prediction. Accessible only by the user specified in the path. Includes 'stockId' for direct reference.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: predictionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific stock prediction event.

definition.description: Represents a user's investment portfolio. Accessible only by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: portfolioId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's portfolio.

definition.description: Represents a single asset holding within a user's portfolio. Accessible only by the user specified in the parent path. Inherits 'userId' and 'portfolioId' from the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: portfolioId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's portfolio.

params.name: holdingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific holding.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated analysis for a user's portfolio. Accessible only by the user specified in the parent path. Inherits 'userId' and 'portfolioId' from the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: portfolioId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's portfolio.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific portfolio analysis snapshot.

definition.description: Represents a single conversation thread with the AI Financial Strategy Advisor. Accessible only by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat conversation.

definition.description: Represents an individual message within a user's chat conversation. Accessible only by the user specified in the parent path. Inherits 'userId' and 'conversationId' from the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific message.

definition.description: Stores structured learning sections. Publicly readable by all users. Write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the learning module.

definition.description: Stores individual lessons within a learning module. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators. Includes denormalized 'learningModuleId' for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent learning module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lesson.

definition.description: Stores quizzes associated with lessons. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators. Includes denormalized 'learningModuleId' and 'lessonId' for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent learning module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz.

definition.description: Stores individual questions within a quiz. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators. Includes denormalized 'learningModuleId', 'lessonId', and 'quizId' for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent learning module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent quiz.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Stores basic metadata for financial stocks. Publicly readable by all users. Write access restricted to administrators/system.

params.name: stockId
params.description: The unique identifier for the stock, typically its ticker symbol.

definition.description: Stores information about financial news articles. Publicly readable by all users. Write access restricted to administrators/system.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the news article.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to signify administrator roles. The existence of a document for a `userId` here grants admin privileges to that user. Read access is restricted to authenticated users to check their own role. Write access is restricted to existing administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an administrator, matching `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for FinIntel AI is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Query Accessibility (QAPs) to ensure robust security rules and efficient data retrieval. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **User-Owned Data:** For all entities directly owned by a user (e.g., `UserLessonProgress`, `StockPrediction`, `Portfolio`, `ChatConversation`), the primary authorization context (`userId`) is embedded directly into the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...`). This ensures that security rules can evaluate `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent document to determine ownership. For example, a user's portfolio (`/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}`) inherently belongs to `userId` from the path, making authorization atomic.
2.  **Nested User-Owned Data:** For entities nested within a user's context (e.g., `Holding` within `Portfolio`, `ChatMessage` within `ChatConversation`), the ownership context (`userId`) and relevant parent ID (`portfolioId`, `conversationId`) are inherited from the parent path. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to verify parent ownership for these subcollections. While the `Holding` document itself might contain `portfolioId` for data integrity, it's not strictly needed for authorization because the path itself validates `userId` and `portfolioId`. To further support potential rule complexity or backend queries, `userId` is often denormalized into subcollection documents as well, though the primary rule relies on the path.
3.  **Public Content References in User Data:** For entities like `UserLessonProgress`, which tracks user interaction with a public `Lesson`, the `learningModuleId` is denormalized directly into the `UserLessonProgress` document. This allows rules or backend logic to quickly access context about the associated public content without needing a `get()` on the `/learning_modules/{moduleId}/lessons/{lessonId}` path, further enhancing independence.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs:**

1.  **Public vs. Private:** Data is segregated based on its security posture. Public, globally accessible content (e.g., `LearningModule`, `Lesson`, `Quiz`, `Question`, `Stock`, `NewsArticle`) resides in top-level collections (e.g., `/learning_modules`, `/stocks`). These are configured for public read access and administrative write access. This segregation ensures that `list` operations on these collections are naturally secure and efficient (QAPs) as they are intended for broad consumption.
2.  **User-Specific Data:** All user-private data is organized under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This clear structural segregation ensures that `list` operations on any of these subcollections (e.g., listing all `portfolios` for a `userId`) are automatically filtered by Firestore's security rules to only return documents owned by the `request.auth.uid`. A user can only query their own `stock_predictions` or `chat_conversations` because the `{userId}` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`, satisfying QAPs by preventing unauthorized data access during list operations without filtering after retrieval.
3.  **Role-Based Access (DBAC):** Administrative roles are managed via a dedicated, simple collection (`/admin_roles/{userId}`). The existence of a document at `/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)` directly grants admin privileges within security rules, adhering to the 'existence over content' principle and avoiding custom claims.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, eliminates the need for expensive and potentially insecure `get()` calls in rules, and provides a clear, debuggable authorization model for the FinIntel AI platform.
